rulings.Case Title: GABRIELA GEYER et al VS. MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 19, 2015, line 1. DEFENDANT HAL 305 HYDE SF, LLC's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to all allegations against the defendants based on duty under B&P 8538 and vicarious/respondeat superior based on that duty. Defendants do not owe any duty, delegable or non-delegable, to Plaintiff pursuant to Business and Professions Code 8538. Cases cited by Plaintiff are distinguishable. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith a fraud-based B&P 17200 cause of action in light of the ruling regarding B&P 8538. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: GABRIELA GEYER et al VS. MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 19, 2015, line 1. DEFENDANT MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to all allegations against the defendants based on duty under B&P 8538 and vicarious/respondeat superior based on that duty. Defendants do not owe any duty, delegable or non-delegable, to Plaintiff pursuant to Business and Professions Code 8538. Cases cited by Plaintiff are distinguishable. Fraud prong is not sufficiently alleged and Plaintiff fails to indicate what amendment is possible. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. VS. JOHN A. PHILLIPS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Carney Family Revocable Trust, George Carney And Ursula Carney, First Republic Bank And A California-Chartered Banks' Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint Per Stipulation And Order Signed On 2/9/15.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 19, 2015, line 2. Defendants Carney Family Revocable Trust, George Carney And Ursula Carney, First Republic Bank And A California-Chartered Banks' Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint SUSTAINED without leave to amend. CCP 405.61. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. VS. JOHN A. PHILLIPS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants John Phillips, Dean Phillips And Aristotle Internationals' Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint Per Stipulation And Order Signed On 2/9/15.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 19, 2015, line 2. Defendants John Phillips, Dean Phillips And Aristotle Internationals' Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to the 8th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to allege all elements of slander of title based on the inclusion of Dean Phillips name on the Grant Deed. Demurrer to the 9th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to re-allege in good faith this cause of action as quasi-contract. Demurrer to the 11th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Michaelian v. State Comp. Ins. Fund (1996) 50 Cal.App.4th 1093. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TED FELDMAN VS. DORIS KOBUCHI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication By Defendants
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 19, 2015, line 3. DEFENDANTs JEAN FONG, WILBERT FONG, DORIS KOBUCHI, ANNA MCCARTHY, SHIRLEY FONG's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. Moving party shifted the burden. Opposing party failed to produce admissible evidence to create a triable issue of fact. Also, Plaintiff's response to the Separate Statement is defective. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANA-MARIA GUERRERO VS. JAMES MCDERMOTT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 19, 2015, line 5. DEFENDANTs JAMES MCDERMOTT and NICHOLAS ANDRADE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Plaintiff's 3rd and 4th causes of action, as alleged, are barred by the statute of limitations. (See paragraph 25 and 32 "Defendant caused Plaintiff to quit the subject property involuntarily and in violation of the Rent Ordinance¿. by their failure to act appropriately to repair the subject property."). Plaintiff shall have 10-day leave to amend to allege in good faith facts re: 3rd and 4th causes of action, e.g. eviction under the pretext of removing the unit from housing use; retaliation; and delayed discovery of facts giving rise to these causes of action. = (501/REQ)
